title: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy
shortDescription: Breathe pure oxygen at elevated atmospheric pressure inside a clear, single-occupant chamber. A clinical-grade recovery modality used by athletes, executives and anyone serious about how they feel tomorrow.

children.text: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy (HBOT) is the practice of breathing pure oxygen inside a chamber pressurised above normal atmospheric pressure. Under pressure, your plasma carries significantly more dissolved oxygen to tissues than breathing at sea level alone. It is a clinically mature modality used in hospitals for decades and is increasingly adopted in recovery settings to support the body's own repair processes.

At Dlús, sessions take place in clear, single-occupant chambers — well-lit, quiet and spacious enough to lie back comfortably with a book or a podcast. Pressure is introduced and released gradually, so most visitors find it gentler than a short-haul flight.

benefits: May support post-exertion recovery and next-day readiness
Users report improved sleep quality and daytime energy
Commonly used alongside strength and endurance training
Research suggests it may support the body's natural tissue repair
A calm, screen-free window for deliberate rest

children.text: Plan around 90 minutes door-to-door for your first visit — 15 minutes for intake, 60 minutes inside the chamber and a short decompression window. Wear comfortable natural fibres; we provide everything else.
You can read, listen to audio, nap or simply close your eyes. A member of the team is within arm's reach throughout your session, and the chamber is clear so you never feel alone.

HBOT is not suitable for everyone. Absolute contraindications include untreated pneumothorax and certain chemotherapy agents. Relative contraindications include recent ear or sinus surgery, uncontrolled high blood pressure, claustrophobia, seizure disorders and some medications.

Before your first session we'll send a short health questionnaire. If anything needs clarification we will book you in for a brief pre-screening call. If you are pregnant or have a serious cardiovascular condition, please consult your doctor before booking.

title: Red Light Therapy
shortDescription: Stand in front of a clinical-grade full-body panel delivering red and near-infrared light. Short, dry, contact-free sessions that slot neatly into a lunch break.

children.text: Red Light Therapy — also called photobiomodulation — uses specific wavelengths of red and near-infrared light delivered through a clinical-grade panel. The light is non-ionising, non-thermal, and absorbed by the mitochondria of your cells. Research is active and promising across a number of areas, from skin and soft-tissue support to general recovery.

Our full-body panel runs at both 660nm (red) and 850nm (near-infrared) — the wavelengths most commonly cited in the peer-reviewed literature. Sessions are dry, contact-free, and short enough to slot into a lunch break.

benefits: Users report support for skin tone, texture and complexion
May support post-exercise recovery and perceived soreness
Commonly used as part of a sleep and circadian routine
Quick, dry and contact-free — no downtime afterwards
Pairs well with HBOT or PEMF on the same visit

children.text: A typical session is 10 to 20 minutes. Eye protection is provided. You'll stand or sit a short distance from the panel with the skin you're targeting exposed.
We dim the room, set a timer and leave you to it. Most guests combine it with a PEMF reset afterwards for a one-hour full-body wind-down.

Red light is generally very well tolerated. Avoid use over areas of active malignancy, photosensitive skin conditions, or while taking photosensitising medications without first speaking to your GP. If you are pregnant, speak to your doctor before beginning any new light-based therapy.

title: PEMF Therapy
shortDescription: Lie back on a full-body mat that delivers gentle pulsed electromagnetic fields. Non-thermal, contact-based and profoundly calming — one of the gentlest resets we offer.

children.text: Pulsed Electromagnetic Field (PEMF) therapy delivers low-level, pulsed electromagnetic fields through a full-body mat. The signal is quiet, non-thermal and imperceptible to most users — but the downstream effect on the nervous system is where it shines. Think of it as a deliberately scheduled window to move your body out of 'fight or flight' and into 'rest and recover'.
Our mats run a range of pre-set programmes from clinical-grade PEMF hardware, tuned for different goals: morning activation, midday reset, or deep pre-sleep wind-down.

benefits: Commonly used for stress reduction and nervous-system reset
Users report support for recovery between training sessions
Often paired with HBOT or Red Light on the same visit
A deeply calming pre-sleep ritual — many guests fall asleep on the mat
Non-contact, fully clothed, no post-session downtime

children.text: Lie down fully clothed for 20 to 30 minutes. We dim the lights, start your chosen programme and let you rest. You'll leave calm, clear-headed and ready for whatever is next.

PEMF is not suitable if you have a pacemaker, implanted cardiac defibrillator, cochlear implant or other active electronic implant. It is also not recommended during pregnancy. Speak to your GP first if you have epilepsy or any known seizure disorder. For everyone else, it is one of the gentlest modalities we offer.
